questions.text: Which of the following support pillars is included in ALL three grant tiers (initGrant, buildGrant, and scaleGrant)?
questions.explanation: Mentorship is the only pillar that spans all three tiers. It begins as weekly office hours in initGrant, evolves into bi-weekly 1-on-1 sessions with code review in buildGrant, and becomes monthly strategic advisory in scaleGrant. Security audit is buildGrant only. Legal entity setup and GTM strategy are scaleGrant exclusives.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: C - Mentorship

Mentorship is the only pillar that spans all three tiers. It begins as weekly office hours in initGrant, evolves into bi-weekly 1-on-1 sessions with code review in buildGrant, and becomes monthly strategic advisory in scaleGrant. Security audit is buildGrant only. Legal entity setup and GTM strategy are scaleGrant exclusives.

Memory aid: Mentorship is the thread that runs through the entire program - it starts on day one and continues through scale.

questions.text: A buildGrant recipient is preparing for mainnet launch. Which support pillar provides a professional review of their smart contracts for vulnerabilities like reentrancy, integer overflow, and access control flaws?
questions.explanation: The security audit is a comprehensive professional review covering reentrancy, integer overflow/underflow, access control, front-running, oracle manipulation, flash loan attacks, gas griefing, and upgradeability risks. Architecture review (option A) focuses on design patterns and scalability, not vulnerability detection. Ongoing monitoring (option C) is a scaleGrant service that watches live contracts post-launch.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B - Security audit

The security audit is a comprehensive professional review covering reentrancy, integer overflow/underflow, access control, front-running, oracle manipulation, flash loan attacks, gas griefing, and upgradeability risks. Architecture review (option A) focuses on design patterns and scalability, not vulnerability detection. Ongoing monitoring (option C) is a scaleGrant service that watches live contracts post-launch.

questions.text: A scaleGrant recipient wants to bootstrap a trading market for their newly launched project token. Which support pillar specifically addresses this need?
questions.explanation: The liquidity incentives pillar specifically covers liquidity pool seeding (pairing your token with MST on a DEX), yield farming campaign design (attracting LPs with sustainable rewards), and airdrop strategy (distributing tokens to genuine early users). BD introductions (option A) help with partnerships but don't directly seed liquidity. Legal entity setup (option B) handles corporate structure, not market making.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: C - Liquidity incentives

The liquidity incentives pillar specifically covers liquidity pool seeding (pairing your token with MST on a DEX), yield farming campaign design (attracting LPs with sustainable rewards), and airdrop strategy (distributing tokens to genuine early users). BD introductions (option A) help with partnerships but don't directly seed liquidity. Legal entity setup (option B) handles corporate structure, not market making.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about the estimated non-cash value of bundled grant services is CORRECT?

options.text: The non-cash value of an initGrant is approximately $25,000.
The non-cash value of a buildGrant is approximately $25,000 - two and a half times the $10,000 cash grant.
The non-cash value of a scaleGrant is approximately the same as its $50,000 cash value.
Only the scaleGrant includes any non-cash services at all.

questions.explanation: The buildGrant's bundled services (security audit ~$15,000, architecture review ~$3,000, UI/UX budget ~$2,000, mentorship ~$5,000) total approximately $25,000 in market value - two and a half times the $10,000 cash. The scaleGrant's non-cash value is even higher at ~$80,000+. Option A is wrong because initGrant has minimal bundled services (~$2,000). Option C underestimates scaleGrant. Option D is false - all tiers include some non-cash support, notably mentorship.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B - buildGrant's non-cash value is ~$25,000.

The buildGrant's bundled services (security audit ~$15,000, architecture review ~$3,000, UI/UX budget ~$2,000, mentorship ~$5,000) total approximately $25,000 in market value - two and a half times the $10,000 cash. The scaleGrant's non-cash value is even higher at ~$80,000+. Option A is wrong because initGrant has minimal bundled services (~$2,000). Option C underestimates scaleGrant. Option D is false - all tiers include some non-cash support, notably mentorship.

Key principle: When evaluating grant value, always add the estimated market cost of bundled services to the cash amount.

questions.text: A project has completed buildGrant, launched on mainnet, and has 300 active users. The founder wants help incorporating a Singapore entity, drafting Terms of Service, and getting introduced to a VC. Which single grant track provides ALL three of these supports?

options.text: initGrant - it covers all foundational needs.
buildGrant - it includes legal support and BD intros for mainnet projects.
scaleGrant - it is the only tier that bundles legal entity setup AND BD introductions.
Any tier - the founder can request specific services regardless of the tier applied for.

questions.explanation: Legal entity setup (incorporation, ToS drafting, token regulatory assessment) and BD introductions (VC warm intros, enterprise partnerships, exchange listings) are exclusively scaleGrant pillars. buildGrant does not include them - it focuses on technical readiness (audit, architecture, UI/UX). initGrant is for concept-stage projects. Option D is false because support pillars are tier-specific and cannot be mixed and matched arbitrarily. The progressive tier structure ensures services are delivered at the appropriate project maturity level.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: C - scaleGrant

Legal entity setup (incorporation, ToS drafting, token regulatory assessment) and BD introductions (VC warm intros, enterprise partnerships, exchange listings) are exclusively scaleGrant pillars. buildGrant does not include them - it focuses on technical readiness (audit, architecture, UI/UX). initGrant is for concept-stage projects. Option D is false because support pillars are tier-specific and cannot be mixed and matched arbitrarily. The progressive tier structure ensures services are delivered at the appropriate project maturity level.

Trap alert: Builders often assume they can request any service at any tier. The grant program is structured to provide the right support at the right stage - not everything upfront.
